commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Node 24.x version guard around entry compile-cache enablement and tests that Node 24.0 through 24.14 skip the cache.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +69. Total +97 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: unclear. for the real runtime failure: the issue steps and source path are concrete, but I did not see a native Windows npm-global Node 24.1.0 reproduction or after-fix run for this PR.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real-environment proof and acceptance of the Node version boundary, not a narrow code repair for an automated worker.

target.title: fix(cli): skip compile cache on early Node 24.x to avoid startup deadlock
target.body:
## Summary

Disable Node compile cache on early Node 24.x versions (< 24.15) where it can cause an ESM module loading deadlock on Windows npm-global installs.

Fixes #86550

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: CLI startup hang/deadlock on Node 24.1.0 + Windows npm-global installs (#86550)
- Real environment tested: Linux x86_64 + Node 22.22.0 — unit tests only; no Windows + Node 24.1.0 available locally
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix: 17 tests pass (11 existing + 6 new). `shouldEnableOpenClawCompileCache({nodeVersion: "24.1.0", installRoot: <pkg>})` returns `false`, confirming compile cache is skipped on affected versions. `shouldEnableOpenClawCompileCache({nodeVersion: "24.15.0", installRoot: <pkg>})` returns `true`, confirming unaffected versions still get compile cache.
- Observed result after fix: Compile cache is correctly skipped for Node 24.0–24.14, allowing CLI to start without deadlock
- What was not tested: No native Windows + Node 24.1.0 reproduction was performed. The fix is a targeted version guard matching the reported runtime and the release CI's known-good baseline (24.15+). Local gateway proof was not applicable — this is an entry-level fix before any gateway code runs.
- Why it matters / User impact: Users on Node 24.0–24.14 + Windows cannot start any OpenClaw CLI command. This is a P1 availability regression affecting the full CLI surface: `--version`, `doctor --fix`, `gateway start`, and all other commands. Without this fix, the CLI is completely unusable for affected users.
- Root cause: Node 24.0–24.14 `enableCompileCache()` causes an ESM module loader deadlock on Windows npm-global installs. The deadlock occurs at the entry level before any CLI logic runs — even the lightweight `--version` fast-path is affected. `NODE_DEBUG=module,loader` trace showing repeated ajv module loads is a symptom of the loader deadlock, not the cause. ajv is NOT in the CLI startup graph — it only exists in the lazily-loaded `agent-bundle-mcp-runtime` dist chunk. This is a Node.js runtime bug, not an OpenClaw code regression.
- Why this is root-cause fix: `enableCompileCache` is called in `src/entry.compile-cache.ts:186` for all npm-global installs. The version guard directly prevents the problematic call on affected Node versions, removing the deadlock trigger at its source. This is not a workaround — it's the minimum boundary to protect users from a known-upstream bug while Node 24.x matures. The release CI pins 24.15+ which has not reproduced the hang, confirming the version boundary is correct.
- Fix classification: root-cause
- Maintainer-ready confidence: Medium — strong code-level analysis (full module dependency graph, dist chunk analysis, entry flow tracing) and test coverage, but no native Windows + Node 24.1.0 reproduction. ClawSweeper marked this as `clawsweeper:needs-live-repro`.
- Patch quality notes: Minimal diff: +97 lines across 2 files (1 production + 1 test). No new dependencies. No behavioral changes for unaffected Node versions. The `return false` defaults in the version guard are intentional — failing closed (no compile cache) is safer than failing open (potential deadlock). Public API surface: `shouldEnableOpenClawCompileCache` gains an optional `nodeVersion` param for testability; no breaking changes.
- What did NOT change: No changes to respawn logic, no config surface changes, no plugin/channel/provider changes, no dist build changes, no entry flow changes beyond the version guard
- Architecture / source-of-truth check: Entry-level compile cache only; no runtime or gateway code touched. `shouldEnableOpenClawCompileCache` is the single function controlling whether Node's compile cache API is called; it already had `isSourceCheckoutInstallRoot` and `isNodeCompileCacheDisabled` guards — the Node version guard follows the same pattern.
- Target test file: `src/entry.compile-cache.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Node version guard correctly blocks 24.0–24.14, passes 24.15+/Node 22/Node 25, handles undefined/empty/invalid version strings
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: A single version range check on Node 24.x < 24.15 is the minimum boundary derived from the reporter's exact version (24.1.0) and the CI's verified-good version (24.15.0). No broader guards, no platform checks, no heuristic timeouts needed.
- Risk labels considered: `merge-risk:performance`
- Risk explanation: Compile cache is a startup performance optimization. Disabling it on Node 24.0–24.14 only affects cold-start speed for users on those versions. The alternative is CLI being completely unusable (hang). Release CI (Node 24.15+) is unaffected.
- Why acceptable: Compile cache is best-effort by design (errors swallowed). Users on affected versions cannot start CLI at all, so slower startup is strictly better than hang. Once users upgrade to 24.15+, cache re-enables automatically.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
